series_description: Deceased Licenses
retention_statement: Paper: 1 year after death;

series_description: Current applicants for exam can retake failed exam two more times...6 months apart; after third failure must complete 'such additional training as the Board prescribes' sec 4732.11.
retention_statement: Paper: 8 years from receipt;

series_title: ROUTINE REQUESTS FOR APPLICATION MATERIALS OR GENERAL INFORMATION
series_description: ROUTINE REQUESTS FOR APPLICATION MATERIALS OR GENERAL INFORMATION
retention_statement: Paper: 1 year from receipt;

series_description: Information to update name, address, phone, employment, current supervisees per sec 4732.14 (if licensee doesn't renew, latest form becomes part of PSY 2...to be destroyed after 6 years.
retention_statement: Paper: 2 years 2 months after next renewal completed

series_title: APPLICATION MATERIALS NOT REQUIRED IN ACTIVE LICENSE FILE (PSY 1)
series_description: Course Descriptions, dissertation information, Vitas, Correspondence, continuing education data, etc.
retention_statement: Paper: 1 year after licensed;

series_title: CURRENT PROJECTS, BOARD POLICIES, POSSIBLE FUTURE APPLICANTS
series_description: CURRENT PROJECTS, BOARD POLICIES, POSSIBLE FUTURE APPLICANTS.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years from receipt, unless superseded earlier;

series_description: Minutes of Board Meetings
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years from meeting date;

series_title: FILES OF CURRENTLY LICENSED PSYCHOLOGISTS & SCHOOL PSYCHOLOGISTS
series_description: Summary Sheet, Application, Transcript, References, Verification of Supervised Experience.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years after license expires or 1 year after death ; Microform: For license applications scanned to micofilm cards (license #1--#4450), retain for 5 years after license expires or 1 year after death

series_description: Cassette tapes containing the examinations of candidates deemed to have failed the board examination for licensure.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until the candidate re-takes and passes the examination (which cann occur 30 days after a failing exam) or until the recording is deemed to have no further use due to an expiration of appeals process

series_title: PSYCHOLOGIST/SCHOOL PSYCHOLOGIST APPROVED BY BOARD FOR OHIO WORK UP TO 30 DAYS PER YEAR
series_description: Application files for non-Ohio psychologists who apply for the "30-day permit."
retention_statement: Paper: 1 year after expiration of 30-day permit.

series_title: Files of Cases Closed Without Formal Action
series_description: Confidential Investigative Files from Complaints Closed Without a Formal Action of the Board.
retention_statement: Paper: Five years after case closure if file is not re-opened within that time frame secondary to a new complaint, except in the event of death of licensee prior to 5 years

series_title: Investigation Records from Files Underlying Formal Board Actions
series_description: Confidential records exempt from public records law (such as medical records) from investigative files from cases resulting in a formal public disciplinary action of the Board, such as a reprimand, suspension, revocation, surrender, or other publicly available disciplinary action against a licensee or applicant.
retention_statement: Paper: Entire paper file scanned to digital file (such as pdf) on Board storage device and back-up; paper file destroyed after Board staff determines integrity of digital file; retain digital file until death of licensee ; Electronic: Digital file images of shredded paper records shall be retained in until death of licensee

series_description: This series relates to copies of letters and underlying public records sent to non-licensees (individuals or organizations) with notice of non-compliance and directives to cease and desist or otherwise evidence compliance with ORC 4732. These letters typcally relate to the use of restricted terms by non-psychologists or the (sometimes inadvertent) listing of non-psychologists under the heading "Psychologists" in public directories (e.g., yellow pages or web-based listings).
retention_statement: Paper: Scan paper records to digital file (e.g., pdf) on Board storage device; destroy paper after determination made by staff that digital file is complete. ; Electronic: Retain scanned digital files (e.g., pdf) for ten years, when presumed that use will be exhausted.

series_title: Public Record Files Underlying Formal Board Actions
series_description: This series is specific to the files containing records underlying formal disciplinary actions of the Board and deemed "public records." These files contain all publicly available information in the Board''s possession and documenting the activities of the Board culminating in a publicly-available action by the Board, including Orders, Consent Agreements, and Notices of Opportuniy for Hearing.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper documents scanned to digital files (e.g., pdf) for placement on Board server and back-up; paper may be destroyed after staff determines the integrity of digital file by viewing it; All paper files have been scanned and future files shall be scanned to digital file as standard operating procedure and ease of storage. ; Electronic: Retain digital file on Board server and back-up media held in secure place for 5 years following the death of the licensee unless there is an order to preserve the file in excess of 5 years due to pending legal action involving the estate or the file.

series_title: FULL PUBLIC RECORDS OF ENFORCEMENT ACTIONS
series_description: Board Order or Consent Agreement plus underlying investigation correspondence and materials documenting the Board''s activites not excluded from public records law.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper copy after action becomes public; entire paper file scanned to digital file on Board storage device and back-up device; Paper file destroyed after staff determines digital file''s integrity. ; Electronic: Retain digital file for 5 years after death of licensee unless under court order to retain longer.

series_description: Pursuant to state law and board rule, a license cannot be issued until the applicant submits to a criminal background check and the board deems that the results are consistent with licensure. Results are received in paper form in the board office reflecting background checks by the FBI and BCII.
retention_statement: Paper: Background check results are noted as having been received/reviewed via a checkmark in the application file. Retain FBI and BCII results 12 months after receipt in the office

series_description: Sec 4732.22(D) requires registration of unlicensed persons practicing under supervision of a licensed psychologist.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years after termination of supervision ; Electronic: For forms scanned to PDF, retain for 5 years following termination of supervision and

series_description: All informal and/or temporary messages, notes, and correspondence, including email, telephone voice messages, post-it-notes, instant messages, text messages, social media posts, shared agency calendars, and any documents that serve to convey information of temporary importance in lieu of verbal communication.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until no longer of administrative value ; Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrative value

series_description: Includes: internal correspondence (memos, emails, letters from state employees including board members and staff); correspondence from external parties including individuals and organizations seeking information pertaining to the board, its laws, rules, and policies; and, other miscellaneous inquiries. This correspondence is informative (it does not attempt to influence agency policy).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 6 months ; Electronic: Retain 6 months

series_description: Correspondence authored by the board''s executive director or a member of the board dealing with significant aspects of the administration of the agency. Correspondence includes all written memoranda, letters, position papers, and drafts thereof, whether on paper or in electronic (e.g., email) media.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years ; Electronic: Retain 2 years

series_description: Paper or electronic requests for routine information or publications received from the public by the Board office staff and replies from staff to such requests via paper or email.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 6 months ; Electronic: Retain 6 months
